content.cn_text: 你将听到易捷旅行社的一位代表告诉一些顾客去亚瑟岛旅行计划的细节。
content.en_text: You will hear a representative from EasyTravel Travel Agency telling some customers the details of a planned trip to Arthur Island.

content.cn_text: 好吧。
content.en_text: Alright.

content.cn_text: 大家好。
content.en_text: hello everyone.

content.cn_text: 现在，你们都是来了解亚瑟岛之旅的。
content.en_text: Now, you're all here to learn about the trip to Arthur Island.

content.cn_text: 你可能知道，这里的人口约为7000人——这是常住居民，与早期约1.7万人居住在这里的情况相去甚远。
content.en_text: As you might know, the population is about 7,000 - that's permanent residents, a far cry from the early days when some 17.000 people lived there.

content.cn_text: 但人口减少的好处是，这个岛保持了自然景观，所以现在。
content.en_text: But the advantage of a declining population is that the island has maintained its natural attractions, so now.

content.cn_text: 在夏季，随着游客涌向这个可爱的小地方，享受它所提供的一切，人口膨胀到22,000。
content.en_text: in the summer, the population swells to 22.000 as tourists flock to this lovely little place to enjoy all it has to offer.

content.cn_text: 有趣的是，亚瑟岛曾经非常孤立，只有一座摇摇晃晃的木桥将它与大陆连接起来。
content.en_text: It's an interesting fact that Arthur Island was once very isolated, with only a rickety wooden bridge connecting it to the mainland.

content.cn_text: 重型卡车不能通过，这可以从1971年的事故中看到，当时桥断了。
content.en_text: Heavy trucks could not pass over, as could be seen by the accident in 1971, when the bridge broke.

content.cn_text: 这导致了新桥的建造，1972年开始建设，两年后完工，从那时起，旅游业的生意蓬勃发展。
content.en_text: This resulted in the new bridge being built, construction starting in 1972, finishing two years later, and since then,business has boomed for the tourism industry.

content.cn_text: 这得益于20世纪80年代在那里建立的赛车赛道，以及直接从岛上水域捕获的新鲜海鲜的声誉，以及著名的本地野生动物，如考拉和鸸鹋，尽管我想说，人们被他们的胃所控制，真正吸引他们的是食物。
content.en_text: This was helped by the motor-racing circuit built there in the 1980s, and the reputation for fresh seafood, caught directly from the island's waters, and the famous native wildlife, such as koalas and emus, although, I would say, people being ruled by their stomachs, it is the food which really brings them in.

content.cn_text: 现在，旅游巴士很早就出发了——早上6点——这是一个漫长的旅程，但你会很享受从窗户看美丽的风景。
content.en_text: Now, the tour bus leaves quite early — six in the morning — and it's a long trip, but you'll really enjoy just looking at the beautiful scenery from the window.

content.cn_text: 尽管如此，我们理解你在某些时候需要伸展一下你的腿。
content.en_text: Still, we understand that you'll need lo stretch your legs at some point.

content.cn_text: 出于这个原因，我们过去常常在鸸鹋公园野生动物保护区停留，直到亚瑟岛发展了自己的保护区，使得以前的停留有些多余。
content.en_text: For this reason, we used to stop at the Emu-Park Wildlife Sanctuary—until Arthur Island developed its own sanctuary, rendering that former stop-off somewhat redundant.

content.cn_text: 现在，我们在巴斯宾馆停了下来，旁边是非常有趣的蠕虫农场，你被邀请去参观，从而了解这些卑微的小生物。
content.en_text: Now, we stop at the Bass Guesthouse, next to the very interesting worm farm, which you're invited to visit, thus learning all about those humble little creatures.

content.cn_text: 如果你对大型野生动物感兴趣，请记住，你需要支付岛上野生动物园的入场费，所以要做好预算。
content.en_text: And if you're interested in bigger wildlife, please remember that you'll need to pay the wildlife park entrance fee on the island, so budget for that.

content.cn_text: 另一个重要的一点是，不要在巴士上喝免费茶的同时吃太多的补充饼干，因为你应该保持你的胃口，这样你就可以尽情享受岛上一些很棒的餐馆，比如著名的雷吉餐厅。
content.en_text: Another important point is not to eat too many of the complementary biscuits with your free tea on the bus, since you should keep your appetite intact so that you can indulge in some of the great eateries on the island, for example, at the famous Reggie's Restaurant.

content.cn_text: 好吧，你知道亚瑟岛的历史。
content.en_text: Alright, you know about the history of Arthur Island.

content.cn_text: 还有我们在那里旅行的细节，所以
content.en_text: and the details of our tour there, so

content.cn_text: 现在让我告诉你一些关于岛上的景点和景点。
content.en_text: let me now tell you a bit about the specific sights and attractions on the island itself.

content.cn_text: 这是一个小地方，但里面挤满了人。
content.en_text: It's a small place, but there's a lot packed in there.

content.cn_text: 如果你想抱抱考拉，野生动物园是首选之地。
content.en_text: If you want to cuddle a koala, the wildlife park is the first place to go.

content.cn_text: 那是在最东端，一个伸向巴斯海峡的小海角——但要小心，风可能会很强。
content.en_text: That's on the most easterly point, a small promontory sticking out into Bass Strait — but be careful, the wind can be very strong.

content.cn_text: 抓住你的帽子，真的。
content.en_text: Hold on to your hats, literally.

content.cn_text: 到那时，你可能已经准备好吃饭了，你可以选择在雷吉餐厅吃饭，或者在自然保护区吃饭，或者在岛中央的一些餐馆吃饭。
content.en_text: By that time, you'll probably be ready to eat, and you could choose between Reggie's Restaurant, or eating at the Nature Reserve, or, at some of the restaurants right in the middle of the island.

content.cn_text: 如果你想要雷吉的，坐配套的巴士去最南端。
content.en_text: If it's Reggie's you want, take the complementary bus down to the southern tip.

content.cn_text: Reggie's就在港口上，直接从船上取海鲜。
content.en_text: Reggie's is right on the port, taking its famous seafood directly from the boats.

content.cn_text: 多么新鲜啊!
content.en_text: How fresh is that!

content.cn_text: 但是，为了节省时间，你可以向北走到岛的另一端，在自然保护区的餐馆吃饭，然后去那里欣赏美丽的海岸风景。
content.en_text: But, to save time, you could go northwards to the very opposite point of the island, and eat at the restaurant in the Nature Reserve, then go and see the beautiful coastal scenery there.

content.cn_text: 还有什么比骑自行车更好的方法呢?
content.en_text: And what better way to do this than cycling?

content.cn_text: 这里有许多步道，安德森海滩也为骑自行车的人提供了一些美丽的机会，当然也可以游泳和冲浪。
content.en_text: There are many trails, and Anderson's Beach also offers some beautiful opportunities for cyclists, as well as for swimming and surfing, of course.

content.cn_text: 然而，在岛的另一边，在主要城镇买自行车。
content.en_text: Get the bicycles, however, on the opposite side of the island, in the main township.

content.cn_text: 最后，你可能想要一个旅行的提醒或纪念品。
content.en_text: Finally,you might want a reminder, or souvenir, of your trip.

content.cn_text: 显然，岛上到处都有纪念品商店，其中许多在安德森海滩，但旅游商店的主要分布在市中心，就在主要道路上，所以无论你在哪里，每个人都可以进入。
content.en_text: Obviously, there are souvenir shops all over the island, with many at Anderson's Beach, but the main spread of tourist shops is centrally located, and right on the main road so that they are accessible to everyone,no matter where you are.

content.cn_text: 是的，在亚瑟岛，你会发现你想做的一切，甚至更多。
content.en_text: Yes, at Arthur Island, you'll find everything you want to do, and more.

content.cn_text: 现在，你们有什么问题吗?
content.en_text: Now, do you have any questions?

groups.desc:
Questions 11-15
Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

items.title: Arthur Island's summer population is
items.analysis: 文中明确指出，在夏季（in the summer），岛上的人口会增加（swells）到22,000。这个数字包括了常住人口和夏季涌入的游客。题目直接询问夏季人口数量，因此22,000是准确的答案。文中还提到了其他人口数字，但它们要么是指常住人口，要么是指历史人口，不符合题目要求的夏季人口。

items.title: The new bridge was completed in
items.analysis: 新桥的建设始于1972年，原文明确指出"finishing two years later"（两年后完工）。因此，我们可以计算出新桥完工的年份是1972年加上2年，即1974年。这与选项C完全吻合，所以C是正确答案。

items.title: The island is particularly well known for its
items.analysis: 文中明确指出，虽然岛上有多种吸引游客的因素，但真正吸引人们的是食物。说话人使用了"I would say"来强调自己的观点，并用"ruled by their stomachs"这个习语来突出食物的重要性。此外，文中多次提到海鲜和餐厅，进一步证实了食物，特别是海鲜，是该岛最著名的特色。

items.title: There will be a stop-off at a
items.analysis: 文中明确提到现在的停靠点是巴斯宾馆，而巴斯宾馆紧邻一个蚯蚓农场。讲解者邀请游客参观这个蚯蚓农场，表明这是行程中的一个重要停留点。因此，蚯蚓农场（worm farm）是此次旅行中的一个停靠点。

items.title: The tour price includes

options.title: C
options.content: tea and biscuits on the bus.

items.analysis: 正确答案是C，因为原文中明确提到巴士上提供免费的茶和饼干（"complementary biscuits with your free tea on the bus"）。这表明这些项目是包含在行程价格中的。相比之下，其他选项要么需要额外付费，要么没有明确说明是否包含在tour price中。

groups.desc:
Questions 16-20
Label the map.
Write the correct letter, A-G, next to the questions.

items.title: Wildlife Park
items.analysis: 答案是 C，因为文中明确提到野生动物园位于最东端，并且提到这是一个突入巴斯海峡的小岬角。地图上唯一符合这个描述的位置是 C。

items.title: Reggie's Restaurant
items.analysis: 因为听力原文明确提到Reggie's Restaurant位于岛的南端（southern tip），并且在港口（port）旁边。地图上标注为G的位置正好符合这个描述。

items.title: Nature Reserve
items.analysis: 正确答案是A。听力原文中提到野生动物园位于“最东边的点”，结合地图中的地理位置，A点正好位于地图的最东边，因此答案为A。

items.title: Bicycle hire shops
items.analysis: 第19题的正确答案是F，因为听力原文中明确提到自行车租赁点位于“岛的另一边，也就是主城区”。因此，主城区就是地图上标记的F位置。

items.title: Main souvenir shops
items.analysis: 正确答案是D。听力原文中提到骑行路线时，描述了这些路线的位置，并提到可以在Main Township获取自行车。地图上，D区域位于岛的中部，靠近主要的骑行路线，符合听力中提到的“trails”和“cyclists”的描述。
